para.emphasis: All text is normative unless otherwise labeled.

listitem.para: The core of XLIFF &version; consists of the minimum set of XML elements and attributes required to (a) prepare a document that contains text extracted from one or more files for localization, (b) allow it to be completed with the translation of the extracted text, and (c) allow the generation of translated versions of the original document.
The XML namespace that corresponds to the core subset of XLIFF &version; is "urn:oasis:names:tc:xliff:document:&version;".
The following is the list of allowed schema URI prefixes for XLIFF-defined elements and attributes:
However, the following namespaces are NOT considered XLIFF-defined for the purposes of the XLIFF &version; specification:
Elements and attributes from other namespaces are not XLIFF-defined.

Any XML document that declares the namespace "urn:oasis:names:tc:xliff:document:&version;" as its main namespace, has <xliff> as the root element and complies with the XML Schemas and the declared Constraints that are part of this specification.
A module is an OPTIONAL set of XML elements and attributes that stores information about a process applied to an XLIFF Document and the data incorporated into the document as result of that process.
Each official module defined for XLIFF &version; has its grammar defined in an independent XML Schema with a separate namespace.

section.title: Changes From the Previous Version

para.emphasis: < Work in progress: XLIFF &version; is still under development by the OASIS XLIFF TC. The list below reflects known differences from XLIFF &pversion; so far and is not yet final. >

section.para: XLIFF &version; introduces the following differences from the previous version published (XLIFF &pversion;):

listitem.para: Added the new Provenance Module, which was designed to store information about the nature and origin of localization data or process, such as the agent, tool, or process that produced or modified a given piece of content.

section.para: For a detailed list of changes made between version &pversion; and the current version (&version;) please see the Specification Change Tracking section in Appendix 2.
Note that all changes introduced in version &version; are designed to maintain compatibility with versions 2.0, 2.1, and &pversion;.
